name: NERSC Perlmutter Public
summary: A public-doc-backed draft adapter for mapping portable skills to NERSC Perlmutter conventions.

job_submit_notes: Set the Slurm account option to the active NERSC project to charge work.
Use -C cpu for CPU-only nodes and -C gpu for GPU nodes.
Quote compound GPU constraints such as gpu&hbm80g in shell commands.
Request GPU resources explicitly with Slurm GPU options when using GPU nodes.
Most production jobs should use the regular QOS; debug is for development and testing.

partitions.name: regular
partitions.purpose: Standard production workload QOS described by NERSC queue policy.

partitions.name: debug
partitions.purpose: Code development, testing, and debugging; not for production runs.

partitions.name: interactive
partitions.purpose: Interactive sessions for development, testing, debugging, and analysis.

partitions.name: preempt
partitions.purpose: Preemptible workloads that can handle interruption through restart or retry.

partitions.name: shared
partitions.purpose: Shared-node work where allowed by current NERSC queue policy.

recommended.module: Review NERSC Shifter and podman-hpc documentation

storage.name: home
storage.intended_use: Small source files, scripts, shell configuration, and personal configuration.
storage.notes: Not tuned for high-performance parallel job I/O.

storage.name: common
storage.intended_use: Software stacks and compiled applications intended to be available from compute nodes.
storage.notes: Use public NERSC filesystem documentation for the current path and compute-node access mode.

storage.name: community
storage.intended_use: Project and collaboration data intended for medium-term sharing.
storage.notes: Project-owned storage; keep permissions and data sharing aligned with NERSC policy.

storage.name: perlmutter-scratch
storage.intended_use: High-performance temporary job working directories and intensive I/O.
storage.notes: Files not accessed within the public NERSC purge window may be deleted; keep critical data elsewhere.

storage.name: hpss
storage.intended_use: Long-term archive for inactive or important data.
storage.notes: Use NERSC archive documentation and allocation-specific quota guidance.

policies.summary: This draft adapter is derived only from public NERSC documentation and should be reviewed by NERSC maintainers before being marked reviewed.
Perlmutter uses Slurm; jobs should specify an account, a CPU or GPU constraint, an appropriate QOS, and explicit GPU resources for GPU work.
Debug QOS is for code development, testing, and debugging; production runs should use an appropriate production QOS.
Use Perlmutter scratch for active, data-intensive job I/O, but treat it as temporary and purged storage.
Use NERSC DTNs and Globus for significant data movement into and out of NERSC.
NERSC documents Shifter and podman-hpc container workflows; adapt generic Apptainer skills through site-specific review.

skill_overrides.notes: Use --account=<nersc-project>, --constraint=cpu or --constraint=gpu, and a NERSC QOS such as debug or regular.
Use the interactive QOS where appropriate and keep interactive allocations short and policy-compliant.
Request GPU nodes with --constraint=gpu and explicitly request GPU resources such as --gpus-per-node.
Prefer the NERSC DTN Globus endpoint for large transfers involving NERSC filesystems.
Stage active job inputs and outputs through $SCRATCH or $PSCRATCH, then copy important outputs to durable storage.
Review NERSC Shifter and podman-hpc container documentation before adapting generic Apptainer examples.
Run storage smoke tests only with site approval and on approved scratch or test locations.
